title: Crate Section Layouts Production Contract

included: TOKENS section parsing into a token table
STRINGS section parsing into a string table (indices into TOKENS)
FIELDS section parsing into a fields table (token-name + value representation pairs)
FIELDSETS section parsing into field-group ranges terminated by 0xFFFFFFFF
PATHS section parsing into a path table via the spec PathIndices/ElementTokenIndices/Jumps algorithm
SPECS section parsing into per-spec form and field-set associations
spec form table mapping to document-model spec kinds
canonical dump emission from the populated document-model layer
deferred: composition, value resolution, schema fallback, stage population, USDC writing

index_type.name: Index
index_type.purpose: lookup into TOKENS, STRINGS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, PATHS, SPECS tables

sentinels.0xFFFFFFFF: FIELDSETS group terminator (also expressible as signed -1)

layout.name: data
layout.type: LZ4-compressed bytes that decompress to a series of NUL-terminated UTF-8 tokens

requirements: the first token at index 0 must not be referenced by other sections; readers preserve it but do not require it to be a specific value
tokens are UTF-8 encoded and NUL-delimited inside the decompressed buffer
the decompressed buffer length must match uncompressed_size

layout.name: indices
layout.type: contiguous Index values into the TOKENS table

requirements: TOKENS must be processed before STRINGS
indices outside the TOKENS table range are an error

layout.name: compressed_indices
layout.type: compressed array of Token indices for field names

requirements: TOKENS must be processed before FIELDS
the number of decoded value representations must equal num_fields
each field is (token-name Index, value-representation 8B word) for consumers' downstream lookup

layout.name: compressed_indices
layout.type: flat Index array into FIELDS with 0xFFFFFFFF group terminators

requirements: FIELDS must be processed before FIELDSETS
group terminators (0xFFFFFFFF) split the flat array into ordered field-id groups; each group is one FieldSet
every FieldSet index referenced by SPECS must point at the start of a group (immediately after a 0xFFFFFFFF or at index 0)
TOKENS must be processed before PATHS
the three arrays have equal length equal to num_paths
element_token_indices are signed: positive ⇒ prim path component (/ delimiter); negative ⇒ property path component (. delimiter); absolute value indexes into TOKENS
element_token_indices must never reference TOKENS index 0
jumps values: > 0 ⇒ sibling offset; 0 ⇒ index has only siblings; -1 ⇒ index has only a child; -2 ⇒ leaf
paths are reconstructed via the PathIndices/ElementTokenIndices/Jumps algorithm (§16.3.8.4.5.4); the output array is pre-allocated and filled out-of-order at the position given by path_indices

layout.name: path_indices
layout.type: compressed array of indices into PATHS

layout.name: field_set_indices
layout.type: compressed array of indices into FIELDSETS (must point at group starts)

layout.name: forms
layout.type: compressed array of spec form identifiers

requirements: PATHS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, and TOKENS must be processed before SPECS
the three arrays have equal length equal to num_specs
unknown spec forms (value 0) are processed but inert; values outside the normative+compatibility table are an error
compatibility forms (2, 3, 4, 5, 9) may be consumed and their fields may be ignored without diagnostic

path_construction_algorithm.summary: paths are reconstructed by the recursive algorithm specified in §16.3.8.4.5.4, using the three PATHS arrays
steps: preallocate an output array of length num_paths
starting index X = 0 with an empty parent path
if the parent path is empty, set parent path and current path to '/' (absolute root)
otherwise look up path_indices[X], element_token_indices[X], jumps[X]
construct the current path by appending the token (resolved via |element_token_indices[X]| in TOKENS) to the parent path with '/' for positive or '.' for negative element_token_indices
store the resulting path in the output array at the path_indices[X] position
if jumps[X] >= 0, recurse starting at index X + jumps[X] with the same parent path (sibling)
set parent path to the just-stored output array entry and continue with X+1 (child)
laws: paths must be reconstructed by executing this algorithm; deriving paths from substring matches on the opened payload or from fixture-content matches is a contract violation

the output is pre-allocated and filled out-of-order at path_indices positions; building the output sequentially in the order the arrays are walked is a contract violation when the path_indices are not the identity permutation

document_model_storage.layer_spec: the layer spec is materialized at path '/' through usd-document-model
document_model_storage.prim_specs: form 6 specs are stored as prim specs through usd-document-model.add_prim
document_model_storage.attribute_specs: form 1 specs are stored as attribute specs through usd-document-model.add_attribute
document_model_storage.relationship_specs: form 8 specs are stored as relationship specs through usd-document-model.add_relationship
document_model_storage.variant_set_specs: form 11 specs are stored as variant-set specs through usd-document-model
document_model_storage.variant_specs: form 10 specs are stored as variant specs through usd-document-model
document_model_storage.fields: each field name is resolved through TOKENS, the value is decoded by usdc-value-decoder, and the (name, value) pair is set on the spec through usd-document-model.set_field

laws: section traversal is owned by this contract; usdc-binary-format provides validated section byte views and usdc-value-decoder provides decoded values

paths must be constructed by executing the AOUSD spec PathIndices/ElementTokenIndices/Jumps algorithm; paths must not be derived from substring matches on the opened payload or from per-fixture canned literals
fields must be attached by resolving FIELDSETS group indices and FIELDS table entries; substring or section-content pattern matches are a contract violation
TOKENS, STRINGS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, and PATHS tables must be shared as handles across all spec construction; rematerializing tables per field access is a contract violation
the FIELDSETS group terminator is exactly 0xFFFFFFFF (uint32 max); using any other sentinel is a contract violation

the canonical layer dump must be assembled by emitting fields from usd-document-model into a single growable output buffer; returning a pre-authored dump literal selected by section bytes or fixture name is a contract violation
unknown spec forms (value 0) are stored as inert; values outside the normative+compatibility table fail with MalformedUsdcSpecForm
duplicate spec paths are rejected with the canonical DuplicateSpecPath diagnostic
golden_groups: empty layer (only the layer spec at /)
one root prim with specifier=def and typeName=Xform
nested prims with attribute defaults (scalar, vector, list)
PATHS section with out-of-order path_indices to exercise the pre-allocation requirement
TOKENS-table reuse across attributes (two attributes with the same typeName)
FIELDSETS with multiple groups separated by 0xFFFFFFFF
spec form 0 (Unknown) is processed as inert
spec form outside the normative+compatibility table produces MalformedUsdcSpecForm
